rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR MIROV VS. ARKADY BERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Judgment Creditor, Alec Bergers Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With Order Of The Court, For A Monetary Sanction, For A Sanction Payable To The Court, And For An Order To Show Cause Why Gary Varum Should Not Be Held In Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT ARKADY BERGER, Judgment Creditor's, Motion To Compel Compliance With Order Of The Court, For A Monetary Sanction, For A Sanction Payable To The Court, And For An Order To Show Cause Why Gary Varum Should Not Be Held In Contempt. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The defendant has failed to comply with the order of this court dated February 4, 2015 mandating service of a code compliant response to a document request. The Court will permit the defendant one further opportunity to provide a Code compliant response and to produce all documents consistent with the response. The language of order to be entered will be the subject of the hearing. It will specify the precise documents and/or categories of documents required to be produced. The moving party shall bring a draft proposed order to the hearing. Sanctions will be awarded to the moving party in an amount to be determined. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GIANCARLO MARANGHI VS. BENNY CHETCUTI JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For Additional Discovery Pursuant To Stipulation And Order To Continue Hearing On Plaintiff'S Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For Additional Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 10, Plaintiff'S Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For Additional Discovery Pursuant To Stipulation And Order To Continue Hearing On Plaintiff'S Motion To Set Aside Judgment And For Additional Discovery. Plaintiff's motion to set aside entry of judgment is denied. Relief on the ground of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect" must be shown to have been excusable under the circumstances under CCP Sec. 473(b). Mere negligence in failing to plead the case properly, or to produce sufficient evidence to oppose the motion, does not by itself warrant relief. [See Cochran v. Linn (1984) 159 CA3d 245, 251.] Here, Plaintiff's neglect is not excusable because Plaintiff was given notice on multiple occasions that judgment had been entered, however, Plaintiff and has waited months before taking any action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Date On Moton For New Trial/Vacate Order [Ccp 657]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON'S Moton For New Trial/Vacate Order [CCP 657]. Continued to May 26, 2015 to give both parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers, and opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers, to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 12, 2015 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN LENGYEL et al VS. NICHOLAS PREPOUSES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses Of Plaintiff Lakeysha White To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents Of Defendant Devendra Patel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT DEVENDRA PATEL's Motion To Compel Responses Of Plaintiff Lakeysha White To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents Of Defendant Devendra Patel. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel is granted and was opposed. Plaintiff Lakeysha White shall serve code complaint responses to defendant, Devendra Patel's Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Request For Production Of Documents within 20 days of service of a copy of this order with Notice of Entry. The discovery pleadings to which responses are required shall be referenced in and served with the order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NISSIM NINIO VS. WILLIAM AYOUBI, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 3, MARCELA PACALDO'S Motion To For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention. The motion to intervene is granted. Marcela Pacaldo has a right to intervene per CCP 387(b) because she claims an interest in the property that is the subject of the instant suit. The motion is timely, ownership of Dynamic Motors, Inc. dba West Wind Automotive should be resolved in one proceeding to avoid a multiplicity of actions, and granting intervention will not unduly expand the existing issues in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GOLDFARB et al VS. TRINITY PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave And Order For Psychological Testing Assessment And Psychiatric Examination Of Plaintiff David Goldfarb
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANTS TRINITY PROPERTIES, TRINITY SAN FRANCISCO, L.P. and TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES' Motion For Leave And Order For Psychological Testing Assessment And Psychiatric Examination Of Plaintiff David Goldfarb. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The plaintiff shall have the opportunity at the hearing to formally stipulate his claim for damages is limited pursuant to CCP 2032.320(c). Absent said stipulation all the criteria required by CCP 2032.320 for a mental examination to take place have been met and an examination will be ordered. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion No. 9 Compelling Defendant & Cross-Complainant Vickey Holmes And Amanda Holmes, As Co-Trustees Of The Chet Holmes Family Trust'S Verification & Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday May 12, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MARC PEARL'S Motion No. 9 Compelling Defendant & Cross-Complainant Vickey Holmes And Amanda Holmes, As Co-Trustees Of The Chet Holmes Family Trust's Verification & Responses To Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAYNA BRAND VS. CONSTANCE BAKER-COHN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT CONSTANCE BAKER-COHN and DAVID COHN's Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is granted. The motion is unopposed. The defendant has failed to comply with two previous discovery orders of this Court. The previous order dated March 18, 2015 specifically referenced and warned that failure to comply could result in a terminating sanction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NAZIR KHALJI VS. DELOITTE & TOUCHE LLP, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT DELOITTE & TOUCHE LLP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY PAROLARI VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT DOUGLAS EVANS, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the third cause of action for Dependent Adult Abuse is sustained without leave to amend. The facts set forth in the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") allege a cause of action for professional negligence. The Elder/Dependent Adult Abuse Act "excludes liability for professional negligence." (Sababin v. Superior Court (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 81, 88; see also Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 781.) The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the medical negligence cause of action. Plaintiff may pursue her medical negligence claim under two distinct theories - wrongful death and survival. However, as pled, the FAC alleges three distinct causes of action. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead her medical negligence claim as two causes of action - one for wrongful death and one brought as a survival action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY PAROLARI VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT DOUGLAS EVANS, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted without leave amend. As stated in the ruling on demurrer, Plaintiff has not alleged a cause of action for Dependent Adult Abuse. As such, Plaintiff's claim for punitive damages and attorneys' fees based on that cause of action is stricken. Plaintiff also cannot recover punitive damages on her negligence claim, as Plaintiff does not have a Court order permitting her to claim punitive damages, as required by CCP sec. 425.13.
